name: rig
description: rig — a quality-gated AI workbench for Claude Code. Give /rig:go (the main entry) a natural-language task: it classifies the task, runs it in an isolated git worktree, and checks the result against an acceptance gate — the diff only lands in your working tree on an explicit accept. Track parallel tasks with /rig:go board; compose recipes, personas, and knowledge as reusable bricks. scripts/mcp_server.py exposes the same task/orchestrate operations over a stdlib-only MCP stdio server, so an external agent, CI, or another process can drive rig without a live Claude Code session — opt-in, no new engine, accept/discard safety unchanged. scripts/workbench.py record-commit/record-outcome/trace-commit close the loop from accept-time gate predictions to real production outcomes — link a commit to its task, record ok/incident, and reverse-look-up a commit to its original gate verdict with a revert-plan draft (never auto-applied). codex/skills/rig/SKILL.md, codex/hooks.json, and .codex/agents/security-reviewer.toml wire rig into Codex CLI's own native layer (Skills/Hooks/Subagent TOML) alongside the existing skill-symlink and --provider codex paths. scripts/host_adapters.py generalizes the Codex native-layer pattern to any host (Cursor added as a second, validating example) via a single per-host capability/hook-event/degrade table — no new host needs core changes. orchestrate.py run --provider anthropic hits the Anthropic Messages API directly to detect and transparently handle Fable 5's refusal-classifier/server-side-fallback (#297), instead of a gate silently failing on it. workbench.py confidence surfaces drill-measured per-reviewer detection rate as a supplementary signal alongside the pass/fail gate, recording reviewer_confidence into acceptance.json without touching gate logic itself. workbench.py diff inserts an AST-based semantic-diff summary for modified *.py files (signature vs. body-only vs. no-semantic-change), augmenting the text diff rather than replacing it. orchestrate.py mcp-scan statically analyzes the MCP server's own tool definitions across three adversarial lenses (attacker/defender/auditor) for shell/network over-permission, secret exposure, and hook-injection risk, and is wired into validate.py for CI. action.yml packages headless CI usage of orchestrate.py run --isolate as a composite GitHub Action, opening a PR only when the gate resolves DONE. orchestrate.py ab runs the same goal through multiple recipe variants concurrently, each in its own isolated worktree, and compares speed/retries/results. workbench.py new surfaces similar past tasks (Jaccard overlap on a rough tokenization, no embeddings) above the routing banner as a deja-vu hint, and talk-mode captures decisions/confirmed-assumptions/open-questions into a per-task talk-log.md once a task-id exists.
